title: Internet & Memorable Facts – Page 495
section: Computer: Internet, Web, Operating Systems & Memorable Facts

questions.question: LISP stands for:
questions.explanation: Page 495: LISP: List Processing.

questions.question: MICR stands for:
questions.explanation: Page 495: MICR: Magnetic Ink Character Recognition/Reader.

questions.question: MIPS stands for:
questions.explanation: Page 495: MIPS: Million Instructions Per Second.

questions.question: UPS stands for:
questions.explanation: Page 495: UPS: Uninterruptable Power Supply.

questions.question: VDU stands for:
questions.explanation: Page 495: VDU: Visual Display Unit.

questions.question: VLSI stands for:
questions.explanation: Page 495: VLSI: Very Large Scale Integration.

questions.question: WWW stands for:
questions.explanation: Page 495: WWW: World Wide Web.

questions.question: MS Access is a powerful program to create and manage:
questions.explanation: Page 495: MS Access is a powerful program to create and manage our databases.

questions.question: Internet is the network of computer networks with millions of computers attached to it. It was originally developed by:
questions.explanation: Page 495: Internet was originally developed by the US Department of Defence.

questions.question: 'The World Wide Web' was invented by:
questions.explanation: Page 495: 'The World Wide Web' was invented by Tim Berners-Lee.

questions.question: FAT 32 and NTFS are the two major file systems in:
questions.explanation: Page 495: FAT 32 and NTFS are the two major file systems in Microsoft Windows.

questions.question: Websites are files in servers, which are:
questions.explanation: Page 495: Websites are files in servers, which are powerful computers.

questions.question: The collection of all websites is called:
questions.explanation: Page 495: The collection of all websites is called World Wide Web or WWW.

questions.question: Hyper text was first coined by Ted Nelson in:
questions.explanation: Page 495: Hyper text was first coined by Ted Nelson in 1960s.

questions.question: HTML is a markup language used to create Web Pages. It uses commands called:
questions.explanation: Page 495: HTML is a markup language used to create Web Pages. It uses commands called Tags.

questions.question: HTML documents are viewed in:
questions.explanation: Page 495: Text editors are used to compose HTML documents. HTML documents are viewed in Web browsers.

questions.question: Most Popular Mobile Operating System is:
questions.explanation: Page 495: Most Popular Mobile Operating System is Android, followed by iOS & Windows.

questions.question: The correct order of Android versions (old to new) starting from Cupcake is: Cupcake → Donut → Eclairs → Froyo → Ginger Bread → Honeycomb → Ice Cream Sandwich → Jelly Beans → Kit-kat → Lollipop → Marshmallow → Nougat → Oreo → Pie → Android 10 →:
questions.explanation: Page 495: Android versions end with: ...Oreo → Pie → Android 10 → Android 11.

questions.question: Some popular operating systems are:

options.B: DOS, UNIX, Windows, LINUX, Mac OS etc.

questions.explanation: Page 495: Some popular operating systems are DOS, UNIX, Windows, LINUX, Mac OS etc.

questions.question: Most Popular Social Networking Sites include:
questions.explanation: Page 495: Most Popular Social Networking Sites: Facebook, Twitter, Linkedin and Instagram.

questions.question: Speed of Computer (or Clock speed) is measured in:
questions.explanation: Page 495: Speed of Computer (or Clock speed) is measured in Hertz (Hz).

questions.question: Speed of Modern Processors are often measured in:
questions.explanation: Page 495: Speed of Modern Processors are often measured in Gigahertz (GHz).

questions.question: Speed of communication over a data channel is measured in:
questions.explanation: Page 495: Speed of communication over a data channel is measured in 'Baud'.

questions.question: DHCP (Dynamic Host Configuration Protocol) is a network management protocol used on TCP/IP networks to provide:

options.B: Quick, automatic and central management for distribution of IP addresses

questions.explanation: Page 495: DHCP provides quick, automatic and central management for distribution of IP addresses within the network.

questions.question: A Social Network can be represented as:
questions.explanation: Page 495: A Social Network can be represented as graph.

questions.question: IPv6 protocol defines an IP address of:
questions.explanation: Page 495: IPv6 protocol defines on IP address of 128 bit.

questions.question: Junk email is called:
questions.explanation: Page 495: Junk email is called 'spam'.

questions.question: Coding is the process of writing out:
questions.explanation: Page 495: Coding is the process of writing out computer instructions.

questions.question: Four major digital initiatives to push e-education by the govt. are Swayam, Swayam Prabha, National Academic Depository and:
questions.explanation: Page 495: Four major digital initiatives: Swayam, Swayam Prabha, National Academic Depository and National Digital Library.

questions.question: Oracle is a:
questions.explanation: Page 495: Oracle is a database software.

questions.question: Most popular online Shopping Websites include:
questions.explanation: Page 495: Most popular online Shopping Websites: Amazon.com, Flipkart.com and ebay.com.

questions.question: A database is a collection of:
questions.explanation: Page 495: A database is a collection of interrelated data.

questions.question: A relational database is a collection of formally described tables from which data can be accessed in many different ways without having to:
questions.explanation: Page 495: A relational database is organized as set of formally described tables from which data can be accessed without reorganizing.

questions.question: The Windows Explorer program is more efficient for viewing folders in:
questions.explanation: Page 495: The Windows Explorer program is more efficient for viewing folders in Windows.

questions.question: Windows Explorer is divided vertically into:
questions.explanation: Page 495: Windows Explorer is divided vertically into two parts or two panes.

questions.question: The left side pane of Windows Explorer displays:

options.B: Disk drives and folders in a hierarchical order

questions.explanation: Page 495: The left side pane displays disk drives and folders in a hierarchical order.

questions.question: The process of linking text values in a series within a formula is called:
questions.explanation: Page 495: The process of linking text values in a series within a formula is called 'concatenation'.

questions.question: Data processing involves some activities like data computing, data manipulation and:
questions.explanation: Page 495: Data processing involves some activities like data computing, data manipulation and information management.

questions.question: An operating system is an interface between:

options.B: The human user and the computer hardware

questions.explanation: Page 495: An operating system is an interface between the human user and the computer hardware.

questions.question: An operating system manages computer resources and performs:
questions.explanation: Page 495: An operating system manages computer resources and performs different functions.

questions.question: An operating system is responsible for:
questions.explanation: Page 495: An operating system is responsible for process management, file management, etc.

questions.question: Data are raw facts and:
questions.explanation: Page 495: Data are raw facts and figures.

questions.question: Hardware comprises of the physical units of a:
questions.explanation: Page 495: Hardware comprises of the physical units of a computer system.

questions.question: Software is a set of:
questions.explanation: Page 495: Software is a set of programs.

questions.question: Both hardware and software together make a computer system:
questions.explanation: Page 495: Both hardware and software together make a computer system functional.

questions.question: SNOBOL stands for:
questions.explanation: Page 495: SNOBOL: StriNg Oriented symBOLic Language.

questions.question: NICNET stands for:
questions.explanation: Page 495: NICNET: National Informatics Centre Network.

questions.question: OMR stands for:
questions.explanation: Page 495: OMR: Personal Computer Disk Operation System... wait. OMR: Optical Mark Reader/Recognition (from abbreviation table).

questions.question: MOPS stands for:
questions.explanation: Page 495: MOPS: Million Operations Per Second.

questions.question: Websites contain pages called:
questions.explanation: Page 495: Websites contain pages called Web Pages.
